description: Validate fixed-distance constraint motion such as pendulums using anchor/body trajectory, constraint length labels, and continuity checks. A pendulum bob is only the smoke family.
physical_assumptions: A constrained body keeps a bounded distance from a constraint anchor.
Constraint length is a structured physics label, not a visual rope length.
The constrained body moves continuously along an arc instead of teleporting.
Swing direction should change after release for pendulum-style motion.
Contact response is optional and must occur after recorded contact if present.
verifier_rules: Constraint length label is present and positive.
Anchor and constrained body appear in every trajectory frame.
Frame-to-frame body displacement stays below teleport threshold.
Pendulum-style motion crosses or approaches the center line after release.
repair_suggestions: Export anchor/body trajectory and constraint_trace sidecars.
Use a physics constraint joint instead of keyframing a rope visual.
Reduce timestep or solver drift if distance error exceeds tolerance.
